channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Crypto, online betting, and vaping: Welcome to the Trump economy
item.description: From tariffs and Big Tech to impacts on the free market—everyone wants to know what Donald Trump’s return to office means for the business world. To cut through the noise, political scientist Ian Bremmer offers his insights.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: AWS and Anthropic ink deal to accelerate model development, enhance AI chips
item.description: The announcement that Amazon Web Services (AWS) will be Anthropic’s primary training partner confirms rumors of an even tighter partnership between the two companies. They announced Friday that Anthropic will use AWS Trainium processors to train and deploy its Claude family of models.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: NBA Meets Disney: A Real-Time Animated Game on Christmas Day
item.description: NBA partners with Disney and Sony for a real-time animated game on Christmas Day, featuring Beyond Sports tech and a special half-time show.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Supreme Court steps into fight over FCC's $8 billion subsidies for internet and phone services
item.description: The Supreme Court has stepped into a major legal fight over the $8 billion a year the federal government spends to subsidize phone and internet services in schools, libraries and rural areas, in a new test of federal regulatory power.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bitcoin ticks closer to $100,000 in extended surge following US elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin extended its streak of record highs after ticking above $99,000 for the first time. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Risk and Compliance Exchange 2024: DoD’ Stacy Bostjanick, DCMA’s Jennifer Henderson on finding ‘any means possible’ to help small biz with CMMC
item.description: As the Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification program inches closer to reality, DoD prepares defense industrial base for compliance requirements.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: TypeScript 5.7 arrives with improved error reporting
item.description: TypeScript 5.7, the latest planned release of Microsoft’s JavaScript variant with syntax for types, is now available in a production release. With version 5.7, the language’s type system is now able to report errors when variables have never been initialized.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DARPA tries a simple but profound concept to improve cybersecurity
item.description: "This idea of compartmentalization has a realization by breaking systems up into small pieces," said Howard Strobe. The post DARPA tries a simple but profound concept to improve cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: From vision to value: A strategic approach to generative AI adoption
item.description: Generative AI has the potential to redefine productivity, create novel applications, and reinvent customer experience.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Elias: JD Vance’s Bay Area connections all but guaranteed him his veep post
item.description: It's a much more complex story than what happened among Democrats, even if Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz was even more obscure than Vance before last summer.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Elias, The Mercury News

item.title: Risk & Compliance Exchange 2024: NIST’s Ron Ross on engineering protections into cyber-physical systems
item.description: The massive convergence of cyber and physical systems has prompted the need for a framework based on engineering principles, the NIST fellow says. The post Risk & Compliance Exchange 2024: NIST’s Ron Ross on engineering protections into cyber-physical systems first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: CIOs contend with gen AI growing pains
item.description: The road ahead for IT leaders in turning the promise of generative AI into business value remains steep and daunting, but the key components of the gen AI roadmap — data, platform, and skills — are evolving and becoming better defined.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Emperor penguin released at sea 20 days after waddling onto Australian beach
item.description: The only emperor penguin known to have swum from Antarctica to Australia has been released at sea 20 days after he waddled ashore on a popular tourist beach.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI networking a focus of HPE’s Juniper deal as Justice Department concerns swirl
item.description: Amid reports of US regulatory concerns over HPE’s proposed buy of Juniper Networks, the CEOs of both companies talked about the future of the blended entity at this week’s HPE Discover event in Barcelona – and that future includes plenty of AI networking technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Silicon Valley tech boom lifts California’s dreary budget view
item.description: A gauge of the so-called Magnificent Seven mega tech companies — Alphabet, Amazon.com Inc., Apple, Meta, Microsoft Corp., Nvidia and Tesla Inc. — is up 56% so far this year, more than twice the advance of the S&P 500 Index.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: How to protect your deliveries from getting stolen by porch pirates
item.description: Porch pirates are on the prowl this holiday season. Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson has a number of tips to prevent holiday package thefts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOJ asks judge to order Google to sell Chrome
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) is asking a federal judge to order Google to sell off its Chrome browser after the court found the tech giant maintained an illegal monopoly over online search.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: AI-powered dog robot sniffs out invasive fire ants
item.description: Fueled by artificial intelligence, CyberDog the robotic dog is designed specifically locate fire ant nests, which are endangering ecosystems.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI agents are coming to work — here’s what businesses need to know
item.description: AI agents will soon be everywhere, automating complex business processes and taking care of mundane tasks for workers — at least that’s the claim of various software vendors that are quickly adding intelligent bots to a wide range of work apps. Many companies appear to be listening.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google faces scrutiny as DuckDuckGo calls for fresh EU probes into compliance
item.description: DuckDuckGo has urged the European Commission to open three investigations into Google’s compliance with the EU’s Digital Markets Act (DMA), alleging non-compliance with key obligations under the law.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: RHEL AI, JBoss EAP 8 coming to Azure cloud
item.description: Red Hat will be making Red Hat Enterprise Linux AI (RHEL AI) and Red Hat JBoss Enterprise Application Platform (EAP) 8 available on Microsoft’s Azure cloud platform. Both arrangements were announced on November 19.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks that were once the entertainment giant's star performers
item.description: Streaming service Peacock will remain with Comcast, as will Bravo, which provides significant content for the Peacock streaming service, and other assets like the NBCUniversal's studios and theme parks.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US gathers allies to talk AI safety as Trump's vow to undo Biden's AI policy overshadows their work
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has vowed to repeal President Joe Biden's signature artificial intelligence policy when he returns to the White House for a second term. What that actually means for the future of AI technology remains to be seen.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: You’ll soon be able to clone your voice to speak other languages ​​in Teams
item.description: In connection with this year’s Ignite conference, Microsoft has unveiled a new interpretation tool that will be added to Teams in the spring.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple admins: Update your hardware now
item.description: Among the first things Apple IT admins woke up to this morning was news of a pair of actively exploited zero-day attacks in the wild targeting Intel Macs, iPhones, iPads, and even Vision Pro users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How your browser is spying on you: Hidden dangers lurking behind every click
item.description: Internet browsers are useful, but they can be dangerous. Clicking on malicious links can direct you to dangerous websites that steal information or infect your device.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft unveils imaging APIs for Windows Copilot Runtime
item.description: Microsoft’s Windows Copilot Runtime, which allows developers to integrate AI capabilities into Windows, is being fitted with AI-backed APIs for image processing. It will also gain access to Phi 3.5 Silica, a custom-built generative AI model for Copilot+ PCs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Data breach exposes over 56 million clothing store customers
item.description: Breach site confirmed 56,904,909 Hot Topic users' data leaked online. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the company's silence makes matters even worse.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How a wrong Google search can compromise your data and bring law enforcement calling
item.description: Wrong Google searches can not only compromise your device and personal data. They can also bring law enforcement to your doorstep. Hackers are targeting Google searches.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
